_comment: PHRASE ENTRY — copy and extend this template for data_phrases.json. Same shape as glossary_entry.json with two differences: (1) has 'scenario' instead of 'posLabel'; (2) no sortKey field (not computed either — simply absent).
_id: Unique across data_phrases.json. Extend the nearest prefix series. Common phrase prefixes: gr (greetings), q (questions), ds (discourse), eat (food/eating), fv (verbs), g (general).
_scenario: Short string grouping phrases by communicative topic. Used to filter the Phrases screen. Keep consistent with existing scenario strings — check data_phrases.json for the active list before creating a new one.
notes: Literally: it is greatly so. The intensifier -kó:wa (great / big) elevates basic niawén:. Used in most community contexts for genuine thanks. Niawén: alone is lighter and more casual.
_accepts: Include the canonical form, unaccented forms, and any dialect variants or shortened forms that are genuinely used.

notes: The standard Kanien'kéha greeting. Returned in kind — if someone says Shé:kon, you reply Shé:kon.
